授業目的: The objective of this course is for the students to understand how research is conducted in business organizations. This course benefits the students in two ways: (1) it helps the students to conduct their Independent Research Project, and (2) it also enables the students to conduct research in real life business setting to solve related managerial problems.
到達目標: Upon successful completion of the requirements for this course, students will be able to: a) understand and critically evaluate relevant academic and research literature; b) develop data acquisition and management skills for research; c) Evaluate the alternative research methods (qualitative, quantitative or mixed method) for their research; d) develop, structure and present a research proposal; and e) carry out a basic research independently project.
授業の概要・背景: In this course, students will get a complete outline of the research process including discussion on research problem, literature review, research design, hypothesis development, variable measurement, data collection, data analysis and presentation of both qualitative and quantitative data.
授業方法: All lectures are conducted completely face-to-face.  Study materials (ie, PowerPoint slides, exercises, and home assignments) will be uploaded to LUNA one week in advance. Students are encouraged to read the materials before joining the class. It would be helpful to note down the key concepts in each chapter to discuss in class. Teaching methods include class lectures, students' debates in class discussions, assignments, and final reports.   Class lecture: In each class, the instructor will open the discussion on a particular topic by clarifying the key concepts and theories. All students are expected to be prepared for class and actively participate and contribute to classroom discussions.    Review of academic paper: Each student is required to choose a published paper based on his/her interest in any academic journal and write a formal review of the article. The review should start by asking the questions like: Why is the article important? What is the research question? and What is the author's motivation to write the paper? Subsequently, students should also comment on other issues of the paper including research design, theoretical framework, hypothesis development, key findings, and contribution to literature and practice. You need to choose the paper within the first two weeks of the semester. A standard review should be 3 pages on A4 size paper. Final report: Final report consists of the submission of a formal research proposal.

トピック.授業外学習2:
Students have to spend at least 4/5 hours in each week to read the study materials of each class. Students are expected to prepare in advance for each class to participate in class discussion.For reading list of each week, please follow the class outline of each week. You are required to read the related chapter before joining the class.
In addition, writing the review of the academic paper and final research proposal also require the students to read wide range of academic articles and business reports related to their research interests.

Each subject is placed on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. 
By studying these subjects with the DP and CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school.
